key_points: The chapter describes beautiful natural wonders such as the Valley of Flowers and the Living Root Bridges
The Valley of Flowers is located in the Chamoli district of Uttarakhand in the Himalayan region
It is famous for its colourful flowers, rich biodiversity, and scenic landscape
Living Root Bridges in Meghalaya are natural bridges formed from the roots of rubber trees
Local communities guide the tree roots over rivers to create strong natural bridges
examples: The Valley of Flowers blooms with hundreds of varieties of flowers during the monsoon season
Streams, waterfalls, and mountains add to the beauty of the valley
Villagers in Meghalaya train rubber tree roots to form Living Root Bridges
These bridges become stronger as the tree roots grow thicker over time
The natural wonders show harmony between humans and nature
formulas_or_processes: Natural Wonder → Unique natural beauty and biodiversity
Human–Nature Interaction → People work with nature instead of destroying it
Environmental Awareness → Understanding the importance of protecting ecosystems
Conservation → Protecting natural wonders for future generations
exam_tips: Remember the location and features of the Valley of Flowers
Understand how Living Root Bridges are created using tree roots
Focus on the concept of biodiversity and ecosystem balance
Learn the message of the chapter: nature should be respected and protected
